methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $78M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.9x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Vivodyne is a Series A-stage biotech and health company founded by Andrei Georgescu and Dan Huh, having raised $78M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 3 public peers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private-stage companies in the Biotech & Health sector, including Colossal Biosciences, Abridge, and Recursion Pharmaceuticals.

Stage-progression analysis is the primary applicable method, using the $78M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage transitions. This yields an implied valuation of $156M. The peer-median approach is not applicable due to insufficient datapoints within narrow filters (same-stage or adjacent-stage peers did not meet the minimum threshold of three comparable companies). Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied because Vivodyne's revenue is not disclosed, which is standard practice for private companies; while the public Biotech & Health universe shows a median P/S of 6.9x across three tickers, this metric cannot be operationalized without target revenue.

The triangulated valuation range is $133M to $179M, centered at $156M. This estimate rests on a single method and therefore carries material uncertainty around stage-specific valuation multiples and comparability of growth and profitability profiles within the biotech sector. Revenue disclosure or additional disclosure of financial performance would strengthen conviction in any valuation within this range.
